title: A Terrible, Horrible Yesterday
learningGoal: The learner will parse a narrative heavily saturated with the Simple Past tense, identifying both regular ('-ed') and irregular past conjugations within affirmative constraints (no 'Is/Am/Are').
instructions: Read this story about a very bad day. Notice that because everything happened YESTERDAY, almost every action word ends in '-ed' or completely changes its shape (like Go -> Went). There are no 'Is' or 'Am' words here! Answer the 8 questions below.

contentItems.text: Every time you see a word like 'Walked' or 'Went', imagine an arrow pointing backwards to yesterday.

readingText: Yesterday was a very bad day. I woke up late in the morning. I looked at the clock. Oh no! I jumped out of bed. I wanted to catch the bus. I ran fast, but I missed the bus. The bus went away. So, I walked to work. It was a long walk. I worked very hard all day. At one o'clock, I wanted lunch. I walked to a small shop. I asked for a hot sandwich, but they only had cold sandwiches. So, I ate a cold sandwich. It tasted bad. In the afternoon, it rained. I needed an umbrella, but I did not have one. I walked home in the rain. My clothes were very wet. I arrived home at six o'clock. I opened the door to my house. I looked in the kitchen. The kitchen was dirty. My dog looked at me. He looked sad. He wanted food. I opened a can of dog food. The dog ate the food fast. I cleaned the kitchen. Then, I watched television. The show was boring. I turned off the television. I crawled into my bed. I pulled up the blanket, and I slept for twelve hours. I hope today is a better day.
textInTargetLanguage: Yesterday was a very bad day. I woke up late in the morning. I looked at the clock. Oh no! I jumped out of bed. I wanted to catch the bus. I ran fast, but I missed the bus. The bus went away. So, I walked to work. It was a long walk. I worked very hard all day. At one o'clock, I wanted lunch. I walked to a small shop. I asked for a hot sandwich, but they only had cold sandwiches. So, I ate a cold sandwich. It tasted bad. In the afternoon, it rained. I needed an umbrella, but I did not have one. I walked home in the rain. My clothes were very wet. I arrived home at six o'clock. I opened the door to my house. I looked in the kitchen. The kitchen was dirty. My dog looked at me. He looked sad. He wanted food. I opened a can of dog food. The dog ate the food fast. I cleaned the kitchen. Then, I watched television. The show was boring. I turned off the television. I crawled into my bed. I pulled up the blanket, and I slept for twelve hours. I hope today is a better day.
baseLanguageSupportText: कल का दिन बहुत खराब था। मैं सुबह देर से उठा। मैंने घड़ी की ओर देखा। ओह नहीं! मैं बिस्तर से कूद पड़ा। मैं बस पकड़ना चाहता था। मैं तेज़ दौड़ा, लेकिन मेरी बस छूट गई। बस चली गई। इसलिए, मैं काम पर पैदल गया। यह एक लंबी सैर थी। मैंने दिन भर बहुत मेहनत की। एक बजे मुझे लंच चाहिए था। मैं एक छोटी दुकान पर गया। मैंने एक गर्म सैंडविच मांगा, लेकिन उनके पास केवल ठंडे सैंडविच थे। इसलिए, मैंने एक ठंडा सैंडविच खाया। इसका स्वाद खराब था। दोपहर में, बारिश हुई। मुझे छाते की आवश्यकता थी, लेकिन मेरे पास नहीं था। मैं बारिश में पैदल घर गया। मेरे कपड़े बहुत गीले थे। मैं छह बजे घर पहुँचा। मैंने अपने घर का दरवाज़ा खोला। मैंने रसोई में देखा। रसोई गंदी थी। मेरे कुत्ते ने मेरी ओर देखा। वह उदास लग रहा था। उसे खाना चाहिए था। मैंने कुत्ते के भोजन का एक डिब्बा खोला। कुत्ते ने जल्दी से खाना खा लिया। मैंने रसोई साफ की। फिर, मैंने टेलीविजन देखा। शो उबाऊ था। मैंने टेलीविजन बंद कर दिया। मैं अपने बिस्तर पर रेंग कर गया। मैंने कंबल ऊपर खींचा, और मैं बारह घंटे तक सोया। मुझे आशा है कि आज का दिन बेहतर होगा।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: Yesterday was a very bad day.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: यस्टरडे वाज़ अ वेरी बैड डे।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: कल का दिन बहुत खराब था।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: I ran fast, but I missed the bus.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: आई रैन फास्ट, बट आई मिस्ड द बस।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: मैं तेज़ दौड़ा, लेकिन मेरी बस छूट गई।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: I ate a cold sandwich. It tasted bad.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: आई एट अ कोल्ड सैंडविच। इट टेस्टेड बैड।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: मैंने एक ठंडा सैंडविच खाया। इसका स्वाद खराब था।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: I cleaned the kitchen. Then, I watched television.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: आई क्लींड द किचन। देन, आई वाच्ड टेलीविज़न।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: मैंने रसोई साफ की। फिर, मैंने टेलीविजन देखा।

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What word does the writer use to mean 'दौड़ा'?
Why did the writer walk to work?
Find the irregular verb phrase in the text: Did the writer say 'I eated a sandwich' or 'I ate a sandwich'?
What happened in the afternoon on his walk home?
What time did he arrive home? (Write a number)
Does the text say 'I am watched television' or 'I watched television'?
How many hours did the person sleep?
True or False: The text says 'I gone home' rather than 'I went home'.

expectedSummaryPoints: The learner correlates the narrative timeline shift to the pervasive '-ed' suffix.
The learner inherently realizes that irregulars substitute the '-ed' morphology.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You correctly identified the past structures. You successfully decoded words like 'Ran', 'Ate', and 'Slept'!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Remember, we are talking about YESTERDAY. The verbs change. Look -> Looked. Eat -> Ate. Sleep -> Slept.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Just double check your irregular words. It is 'I ate', never 'I eated'. It is 'I went', never 'I goed'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Yesterday was a very bad day... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Scan the text for the letters 'ED'. Notice how they attach directly to the verb without any helper words!
commonMistakes: Thinking 'slept' means something else because it doesn't end in 'ed'.
